intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboards benefit from high contrast in low-light environments where users check their data at night
facade.label: Dark background, bright text
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark mode variants with color-coded metric highlights
facade.rejectImplies: Test light/neutral backgrounds with softer contrast instead

evidence.hypothesis: Health dashboards benefit from high contrast in low-light environments where users check their data at night
evidence.implication: Explore dark mode variants with color-coded metric highlights

facade.hypothesis: The dashboard uses warm, chat-like language in microcopy and notifications rather than clinical or technical terminology
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers voice that feels like a supportive friend checking in on their experiments, with casual phrasing and encouragement
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clinical, objective, or formal language that prioritizes clarity and authority over warmth

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for navigation paired with card-based content layout will feel organized and scannable for tracking multiple health experiments simultaneously
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) + content grouping (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and visual separation between experiments; prefers vertical space for navigation over horizontal tabs
facade.rejectImplies: User wants full-width content, fewer chrome, or horizontal navigation that doesn't compete for screen real estate

facade.hypothesis: Fluid animations between dashboard states (data updates, tab switches, metric reveals) feel more exploratory and engaging than instant visual changes
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity: animated flow vs snappy instant
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: User values graceful motion and visual storytelling in data interactions—embrace easing curves, staggered reveals, morphing elements
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers speed and directness over motion polish—keep transitions minimal, instant feedback, reduce animation overhead

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a single consolidated view of key metrics over time rather than scrolling through individual data points
facade.axisTargeted: AGGREGATION: dense data table vs. narrative summary card
facade.label: Weekly summary card
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick weekly snapshots and pattern recognition over granular daily logs—design should emphasize rolled-up metrics and trend cards
facade.rejectImplies: User needs to see all data points visible at once; aggregation obscures details—design should expose raw data in accessible formats like timelines or detailed tables

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers a warm, inviting atmosphere over clinical data presentation—soft lighting and rounded elements will feel more nurturing for personal health tracking.
facade.label: Soft, glowing cards
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into ambient, comforting design; use warm color palettes, blur effects, and organic curves. Health data becomes less sterile.
facade.rejectImplies: User favors clarity and precision over coziness. Move toward clean, high-contrast, sharp-edged layouts with cooler tones.

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboards need clean, sparse layouts with numbers and simple grids rather than heavy visualization, allowing users to quickly scan their metrics without cognitive overload
facade.axisTargeted: Dense visual information vs. airy simplicity
facade.label: Minimal data tables
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize tabular data display, whitespace, and typography-first design; avoid charts and decorative elements
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer visualization-heavy dashboards with charts, graphs, and visual storytelling to understand trends at a glance

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
